issues.description: WIN-052 DOI 10.3847/2041-8213/ad8e69 returns 404 — does not resolve to any paper. Actual Mistele 2024 ApJL paper has DOI 10.3847/2041-8213/ad54b0. Broken DOI link undermines credibility.

WIN-052 mischaracterizes dome's RAR argument. Dome claims empirical RAR extends smoothly across kinematic+lensing with zero scatter (supporting 'aetheric field'), not that dome has its own lensing mechanism. Rebuttal should address actual claim: dome has no RAR derivation, no galaxy-scale physics.
WIN-052 missing structural self-contradiction: RAR spans ~1 kpc to ~1 Mpc, but dome's universe is capped at 20,015 km disc radius and 9,086 km firmament. Dome has no ontology for galaxies.

WIN-066 FABRICATED DOI: 10.1038/ngeo2516 resolves to Doetterl et al. (2015) about SOIL CARBON, not hemispheric energy balance. A dome defender checking this DOI would discredit our entire review. Replace with Stephens et al. (2012) doi:10.1038/ngeo1580 or Stephens & L'Ecuyer (2015) doi:10.1007/s10712-015-9340-1.

WIN-066 +0.34 W/m² appears to be a decadal TREND (Raghuraman et al. 2025 PNAS), not a static hemispheric difference. Stephens et al.'s famous finding is that hemispheres are remarkably SYMMETRIC. Our review and dome both mischaracterize this figure.
WIN-066 missing dome geometry acknowledgment. Dome IS geometrically asymmetric (NP at center). Should note dome provides no radiation budget calculation translating geometry to W/m² prediction.
WIN-066 finding '2× more land' oversimplifies — the mechanism is differential thermal inertia, albedo, and evaporation, not just land area.

WIN-067 says satellites orbit 'above the alleged dome' but Swarm/GRACE at 250-500 km are BELOW the dome's firmament at ~9,086 km. Should say satellites orbit within dome's predicted field, making the 90% gravity drop even more damning (satellites should measure it directly).

WIN-067 'toroidal sump node' is undefined. Term appears nowhere in dome's published physics. Should explicitly state the dome introduces an undefined mechanism with no equations, predictions, or physical content.
WIN-067 post-glacial rebound explanation incomplete. Primary explanation per recent literature is mantle plume beneath East Antarctic Plateau (Einarsson et al. 2020 PNAS), not just ice sheet rebound.

SEC-3.1 Polaris inconsistency: Bottom line says 'dome is contradicted by data in 1 (Polaris)' but Domain #17 table entry says 'Neither model is tested by this.' Direct self-contradiction in most quotable paragraph.
issues.rationale_for_deferral: DEFERRED: Requires coordinated edit of SEC-3.1 prose in generate-html.js — need to decide which statement to keep.

WIN-047 detail_evidence says Hubble Law verified "z ~ 0 to z ~ 20" — WRONG. Pantheon+ covers z = 0.001 to z = 2.26. Even GRBs reach only z ~ 8-9. z ~ 20 has no observational basis.

WIN-047 missing fundamental objection: dome firmament at ~9,086 km cannot contain galaxies at millions of light-years. Dome has no ontology for extragalactic objects, making redshift claims meaningless.

issues.description: WIN-065: The dome does not specify what instrument was used, how many measurements, at what time(s), under what conditions, how the prediction was calculated, or what the error budget is. A single unreplicated measurement is not confirmation.

WIN-065: If n(r) = 1 + 0.20·(r/20015)² predicts refraction at different latitudes, it produces ~0.04° at Oslo (59.91°N, r=2753 km), not the claimed +4.5°. The formula fails on the dome's own test case.
WIN-065: PRED-NEW-001 was registered 2026-03-12 after dome site changes and development work. It may reflect known measurements rather than prospective prediction.

WIN-065: Combined error from Polaris's 0.74° polar offset, ~0.02-0.03° refraction, and ±0.2-0.5° measurement uncertainty is ±0.3-0.5°. The +0.27° figure falls entirely within this band and is not statistically significant.

WIN-058 itself is undefined on dome's site — listed as one-line claim with no equations, measurements, or physical consequences. Our review argues against our own interpretation, not the dome's actual claim. Weakens argument.

WIN-058 'convention not prediction' argument proves too much — WGS84 is also 'just a convention' but enables GPS precision. Should instead examine whether dome's 0.9941 angular scale factor was derived a priori from dome geometry or fitted post-hoc to WGS84 distances.

WIN-058 Torge & Müller (2012) citation misused. We claim it proves coordinates are 'not testable,' but the book is about making coordinates PRODUCE testable predictions with millimeter precision. Source contradicts our use of it.

WIN-058 verdict 'Misleading' may be wrong. Not cherry-picked, duplicated, or circular — it's a coordinate system. 'Not Demonstrated' may be more accurate (dome hasn't shown any testable prediction from this coordinate system).
WIN-057 and WIN-058 conflated in build doc. Need to determine if they are distinct claims or should be merged.
WIN-058 detail_extra null — could explain coordinate formula, 0.9941 scale factor, comparison with UTM/WGS84.

issues.description: WIN-017: We don't show the math. The dome claims 20m wobble → 0–0.5 arcsec. At firmament height 9,086 km, arctan(20/9,086,000) ≈ 2.2 × 10⁻⁶ rad ≈ 0.454 arcsec. Producing the observed continuous variation from 0.77" (Proxima Cen) to ~0.001" (distant stars) would require each star's wobble to be individually tuned to its distance, which is circular fitting.'

Conclusion vulnerable to: "They never addressed our actual discriminating tests." Fix: Add response explaining Chapman conductivity effects depend on local Sq geometry, not just coverage fraction.

VERDICT LEGEND ORDERING ARBITRARY: Current verdict order in legend appears unsystematic. Recommend ordering by evidential force: (1) Wrong on Own Terms (Self-Contradicted, Refuted by Data) → (2) Not Evidence (Std Model Explains, Misleading) → (3) Not Evaluable (Not Demonstrated, Unfalsifiable). This grouping reflects logical hierarchy: Can the dome contradict itself? → Can the dome offer new evidence? → Can the dome be tested? Current legend order does not reflect this structure.

NOT DEMONSTRATED DEFINITION INCOMPLETE: Current definition covers 'unreplicated fringe experiments' but should expand to include 'claim made without derivation from model geometry' or 'adopted from external source without independent justification.' Many dome WINs adopt published observations then claim them as predictions without showing how dome geometry predicts the specific observation. Example: WIN-054 (El Gordo) notes the anomaly exists, but dome never derives why dome geometry specifically predicts an El Gordo-scale anomaly rather than some other value. Broader definition would make this WIN a natural 'Not Demonstrated' fit.

SEC-3.2: Claims dome "applied his correction factor of 1.672" but 1.672 is KAPPA coupling constant (WIN-012), not the eclipse FSF. Actual eclipse uses station-specific FSF values (0.642-2.075) derived from H(r)/r.

issues.description: SEC-4.9 REFRACTION CLAIM OVERSTATED: Section states "At least six WINs rely on aetheric refraction to avoid falsification" citing WIN-016, 017, 026, 033, 056, 065. Verification of current WIN detail text shows only WIN-016 and WIN-065 explicitly mention aetheric refraction or n(r). WIN-017 (stellar parallax) discusses firmament wobble; WIN-026 (crepuscular rays) discusses perspective effects; WIN-033 (Sigma Octantis) discusses intrinsic luminosity; WIN-056 (solar elevation) discusses globe formula substitution. A dome defender checking the citation would find only 2 of 6 WINs explicitly invoke refraction in our own text. The logical argument (dome geometry WOULD require refraction) is valid but our text does not make that dependency explicit for WIN-017/026/033/056. Section 4.9 requires editing generate-html.js: either (a) add explicit refraction dependency notes to WIN-017/026/033/056 detail text, or (b) soften Section 4.9 to "would require aetheric refraction or equivalent unfalsifiable mechanism."

WIN-053 V50.6 argument reframe: instead of penalizing model evolution, focus on (1) the architectural change was retroactive/unmotivated; (2) new toroidal topology still fails via flux conservation. Curmudgeon notes current framing looks unfair.

SEC-4.5.9 CRITICAL FACTUAL ERROR: Section claims "d_geo is undefined" as the first technical argument. This is FALSE for V13. The dome's V13 coordinates page explicitly defines d_geo: EW_arc = 4×a×E(e²)×(Δlon/360)×(r_avg/a), NS = |r₁−r₂|, d_geo = √(NS²+EW_arc²), where a=20,015 km, e=0.66. A dome defender can immediately show we didn't read their page. This is the lead argument in the section — failing it damages the entire section's credibility.

issues.description: SEC-4.8 MAJOR SELF-CONTRADICTION: Section claims sun appears "50% larger at noon" AND "30% distance variation = 30% size variation" — these are mathematically inconsistent. If the sun is 30% closer at noon than at horizon, angular size increases by 1/0.70 = 43%, not 50%. The actual dome geometry gives a 48% reduction at 90° hour angle (sun at horizon vs. overhead in flat-earth geometry). Our text contains a numerical inconsistency in a section critiquing numerical inconsistencies.
issues.fix_details: Fixed by analyst expansion: EXP-001/003/010 — content integrated into sections.json

issues.description: KILLSHOT-GAIA missing New Horizons parallax experiment (April 2020): NASA measured stellar parallax from 7-billion-km baseline (Proxima Cen: 32.4 arcsec, Wolf 359: 15.7 arcsec). Physically impossible if stars are on a dome 9,086 km high. Baseline (7B km) is ~800,000 times the firmament height; measurement made from beyond any hypothetical dome, eliminating aetheric refraction as escape hatch.

issues.description: KILLSHOT-GAIA missing differential-parallax argument: In any small patch of sky, Gaia measures stars with parallaxes differing by 100-1000x. Any dome motion (rigid wobble, flexible undulation, or refraction) would affect all objects in the same direction similarly. Per-star parallax variation perfectly correlated with independently determined distance is impossible under any dome mechanism.

Better: "Scale length λ = 8,619 km used for both firmament height and magnetic field has no independent derivation."

WIN-055 c2: "geometric tracers calibrated via Magellanic Cloud stellar parallax" is technically imprecise — Cepheids and SBF are standard candles (luminosity-based), not geometric tracers. They are ANCHORED to geometric parallax but are not themselves geometric. Fix: change to "standard candles anchored to Magellanic Cloud geometric parallax".

WIN-055 c2: "Gaia DR3 parallaxes independently confirm Cepheid distances to ~1%" overstates scope — Gaia confirms Milky Way Cepheids specifically (and LMC/SMC anchors). Fix: add "for Milky Way Cepheids" to specify scope and avoid implying Gaia reaches extragalactic distances.

WIN-054 c2: finding conflates two separate critiques: (1) El Gordo is a genuine ΛCDM anomaly (high velocity at formation epoch) and (2) the dome makes no prediction about it. These should be stated separately. The combined statement obscures that we acknowledge the ΛCDM anomaly is real — our point is that dome offers nothing better. Linked to EXP-022.

issues.description: HOL-HIERARCHY (curmudgeon alert 2026-04-07T15:30): Argument hierarchy is inverted for persuasive force. Self-contradiction arguments (Schumann WIN-002, Tidal WIN-045/046/049/050/051, Gravity at rim WIN-067) are in Section 4.5 (Internal Contradictions tab, 8th of 10 tabs). External falsification arguments (weaker — vulnerable to frame-of-reference objections) are in Part 4 (tabs 2-3). Readers encounter the weaker external arguments first and may never reach the dome's own geometry refuting itself. Restructuring Part 4 to lead with self-contradictions would significantly improve persuasive impact without changing any content.
issues.fix_details: Resolved by V6 restructure (commit 6b3e4a0). Self-Contradictions moved to Part 2, immediately after The Model (tab 4 of 11). Kill Shots extracted to own tab (Part 5). All sections renumbered 1-10 matching tab order.

issues.description: Unsourced 30% threshold in WIN-059 detail_evidence. Dome dashboard shows 50% tolerance (Status: PASS at 39.9% error). The dome's own note says tolerance was "originally tighter, widened when predictions missed." Our claim that 39.9% exceeds the dome's "own 30% acceptability threshold" is factually incorrect. Also affects Kill-Shot Test 6 in sections.json part5.
issues.fix_details: Replaced "self-falsified" + 30% threshold framing with tolerance-gaming argument using dome's own dashboard admission. Applied in WIN-059 detail_evidence and sections.json part5 Kill-Shot Test 6.

issues.fix_details: Updated WIN-059 finding to reflect tolerance-gaming argument.

issues.fix_details: Patched sections.json part5 to use tolerance-gaming framing with dome formula prediction (~22.8 km/yr for 2026).

issues.description: Internal inconsistency in WIN-057: finding/detail_verdict_text say "600× worse (sub-0.01%)" but detail_evidence says "60,000× worse (better than 0.0001%)". Factor of 100 discrepancy. Dome defender would exploit this. Also removes redundant OPEN-015 parenthetical from detail_evidence (OPEN-015 has dedicated section in detail_extra).
issues.fix_details: Changed "better than 0.0001%" to "better than 0.01%" and "60,000×" to "600×" in WIN-057 detail_evidence. Removed redundant OPEN-015 parenthetical.

issues.description: MONITORING TAG ERROR: WIN-060 code_analysis.monitoring is "hardcoded" but no hardcoded monitoring check exists for this WIN. The dome's W022 prediction maps to WIN-040 (static position), not WIN-060 (drift direction). The live dashboard SAA domain tracks intensity decay rate (nT/yr), not drift direction. All sibling WINs (040, 041, 043, 059, 063) are correctly tagged "none". WIN-060 should be "none" too.

KILL SHOT 6 USES UNSOURCED 30% THRESHOLD: sections.json part5 (Kill Shots) says "the dome's methodology page states that predictions exceeding 30% error trigger review and potential falsification." The actual dome criterion is 50% (dashboard shows PASS at 39.9%). The 30% figure does not appear in the extracted dome raw-text. Also incorrectly labels the result "self-falsifying" when the dome marks it PASS. Cross-cutting error: both sections.json part5 and previously WIN-059 detail_evidence referenced this unsourced threshold (WIN-059 was already fixed; part5 still has it).

UNSOURCED 40% POPULATION CLAIM: detail_evidence states "~40% of the world's population" lives in the ±12° equatorial band excluded by V13. The tropics (±23.4°) contain ~40%; the ±12° band is roughly half that latitude range and contains far fewer people (~15-20%). Either cite a source or use a more conservative estimate.

SELF-FALSIFIED FRAMING INACCURATE: detail_evidence says "By the dome model's own published falsification criteria, WIN-059 is self-falsified." But the dome's own dashboard shows this test as PASS (50% tolerance, 39.9% error). The correct framing is tolerance-gaming, not self-falsification. Should be: "The dome marks this as PASS by using a 50% tolerance that was widened from an originally tighter value after predictions missed."

POLE APERTURE RADIUS ARBITRARY: The area ratio derivation uses a pole aperture radius without sourcing where this value comes from. The choice appears arbitrary — if it is dome-defined, cite the dome parameter; if we chose it for the calculation, acknowledge that.

FINDING CONFLATES TWO CRITIQUES: "El Gordo is a genuine 6.2σ ΛCDM anomaly" (acknowledging a real problem) and "Citing a competitor's unsolved problem without offering your own solution is non-discriminating" (cherry-picking critique). These are not compatible: if El Gordo is genuinely anomalous, the dome is not cherry-picking; if it's cherry-picking, then El Gordo is not genuinely hard for ΛCDM. The finding should clearly commit to one framing.

NUMERICAL INCONSISTENCY: detail_evidence says "600–700× less accurate" while comparing to "sub-0.01%" elsewhere. The 7.3% NH regression vs sub-0.01% gives ~730×; the 6.2% RMSE gives ~620×. The range "600–700×" is reasonable but should be clearly explained: these represent best-case (6.2% RMSE) and worst-case (7.3% NH) relative to Vincenty at sub-0.01%.

MISSING OVERLAP DISTINCTION: WIN-004 (separation), WIN-005 (decay rate), and WIN-060 (drift direction) share the same INTERMAGNET/Swarm dataset. The text says they overlap without explaining why this constitutes redundancy rather than independent tests. Should add: "independent properties read from the same dataset without independent dome-geometric derivation does not multiply predictive power."

MOND PRIORITY ARGUMENT UNDERREPRESENTED: detail_verdict_text mentions MOND once but buries it. Must explicitly contrast MOND's mathematical content (transition function μ(x), critical acceleration a₀) against dome's zero-equation aetheric label. A dome defender can say "if MOND explains RAR without dark matter, why not aetheric medium?" — the counterargument must be prominent.

GALAXY-SCALE ONTOLOGY UNDERPLAYED: The firmament-at-9,086-km structural incoherence is mentioned once as a secondary point. This is the single strongest argument — the dome cannot physically accommodate galaxies at kpc distances. Should lead the rebuttal, not be buried.

Cross-WIN structural incoherence (WINs 047, 048, 052) not explicitly called out: these WINs test the same underlying claim (RAR/galaxy dynamics) from different angles without independent dome-geometric derivations. A reviewer should note this overlap explicitly.

MOND vs ΛCDM framing confusion: rebuttal text conflates two separate arguments — (1) MOND has priority over dome on the RAR, and (2) ΛCDM's difficulties don't help the dome. These should be clearly separated.

WIN-055 and WIN-047 duplication: both WINs test essentially the same observable (distance ladder calibration) from overlapping data. This redundancy has not been explicitly called out in the text despite being flagged in Cycle 1.

MISSING CROSS-REFERENCE: detail_evidence for WIN-053 makes a claim that is supported by analysis in Section 6.8 (old 3.5.8) on flux conservation. The cross-reference is absent, leaving the argument without backup.

WIN-061 citation imprecise: detail_evidence cites Nickolaenko & Hayakawa 2002 ch.11 for storm-specific Schumann suppression evidence, but Schlegel & Füllekrug (1999, J. Geophys. Res. 104, 10111) is the primary peer-reviewed source specifically documenting G3+ storm suppression of Schumann frequencies. The general reference should be supplemented or replaced with the storm-specific one.

WIN-061 Live Power Dashboard untapped: the dome's own Live Power Dashboard lists WIN-061 as "Unscored — no G3+ storm observed." This is a powerful self-undermining argument: the dome claims confirmation of a G3+ storm suppression test, but its own monitoring infrastructure confirms no qualifying storm has been observed. detail_evidence should add: "The dome's own Live Power Dashboard scores this WIN as Unscored — meaning by its own automated metrics, the test has never been run."

EXHIBIT-AB COVERAGE GAP: The dome's home page prominently features Evidence Exhibits A and B (2003 Bedrock Audit of BOU station and Administrative Mask Exposure). These are the first things visitors see, yet our review has no dedicated section addressing them. Visitors who arrive from the dome site's home page will find our rebuttal silent on the dome's most prominent claims.

EXHIBIT-AB STATISTICAL CLAIMS UNADDRESSED: The dome's Exhibit A prominently cites r=0.992 correlation and AI=1.152 asymmetry index as key evidence. These specific statistics are never addressed in our review. Any reader familiar with the dome site will notice this gap.

Kim et al. citation in detail_evidence is listed as 2021 (ApJ 923, 101) — Cycle 1 raised concern about this citation's accuracy. The DOI (10.3847/1538-4357/ac294f) should be verified to confirm it corresponds to a paper about El Gordo mass measurements and that the mass figure cited (2.13×10^15 M_sun) appears in that paper.
issues.fix_details: DOI verified correct: 10.3847/1538-4357/ac294f is Kim et al. 2021 ApJ 923, 101 (Head-to-Toe Measurement of El Gordo). Paper is about El Gordo mass. Risk low per investigation notes — our argument does not hinge on the specific mass value.
issues.investigation_notes: DOI 10.3847/1538-4357/ac294f VERIFIED: IS Kim et al. 2021 ApJ 923, 101 (Head-to-Toe Measurement of El Gordo). IS about El Gordo cluster. Their reported subcluster masses: M_NW=9.9e14 + M_SE=6.5e14 ≈ 1.64e15 M_sun combined. The 2.13e15 M_sun figure cited in our text may be from the total system mass measurement in the same paper. DOI is correct; paper is correct; mass figure needs full text verification. Risk low — our argument does not hinge on the specific mass value.

issues.description: Aubert et al. (2013, doi:10.1038/nature12574) cited for hemispheric asymmetry in detail_evidence — but paper may be about inner core control of secular variation, not hemispheric decay asymmetry specifically. A dome defender with paper access could challenge whether it shows what we claim. Curmudgeon Cycle 2 recommends adding Olson & Deguen (2012, doi:10.1038/ngeo1506) as more targeted citation.

issues.description: WIN-063 detail_evidence has malformed nested <a> tag: the fix for ISS-646 introduced an outer <a href="nature12574"> that wraps the Olson & Deguen citation incorrectly, creating invalid HTML. The outer anchor needs to be removed, leaving only the distinct Olson & Deguen (ngeo1506) and Aubert et al. (nature12574) links.

WIN-063 detail_evidence uses "1.4×" as an example ratio value. This was invented by us as an illustrative example and could be misread or quoted out of context by a dome defender as a value we endorse. Cycle 2 curmudgeon recommendation: replace with a variable (e.g., "X%") to avoid creating a false attack surface.

Wrong DOI for Chapman 1933. Link points to DOI 10.1098/rsta.1933.0001 (Phil. Trans. Royal Society A) but Chapman's 1933 eclipse paper was in Terrestrial Magnetism and Atmospheric Electricity 38(3), not Phil. Trans. A wrong DOI for the foundational citation exposes us to a fabrication accusation.

Unverified dome quotes in WIN-068: text quotes dome saying 'honest caveat' and 'Globe has a competing ionospheric conductivity mechanism' and 'Tier 1 is consistent with both models' — these phrases do not appear in our V51.0 raw-text captures and may be inaccurate paraphrases of V51.1 Steelman Evidence Ladder content. Risk of strawman accusation.

Ashour-Chapman link mislabeled. Text says 'the Ashour-Chapman conductivity model' and links to a 2021 Frontiers review paper (10.3389/fspas.2021.766327), implying that paper IS the model. The original Ashour-Chapman framework is from Ashour 1950/Chapman 1919. Also: claim that it 'quantitatively predicts' is overstated — the model predicts qualitative patterns, exact magnitudes vary by eclipse.
detail_group inconsistency: WIN-068 has detail_group WIN-010/025/068 but WIN-010 and WIN-025 still have WIN-010/025. All three should reference the full group.
Finding field buries the triple-counting argument, which is the strongest point. Reader scanning summary table only sees citation of Kim & Chang 2018 mechanism, not the duplication with WIN-010/025.

Nullarbor ratio unverified. WIN-069 states "ratio of 1.00" for Nullarbor Highway but curmudgeon computation gives ~1.07 for the full Norseman-Ceduna stretch. 1.00 may refer only to the dead-straight 146 km central segment. Needs clarification.

WIN-069 does not preempt strongest dome defense: "Globe also cannot predict road distances — Vincenty gives geodesics not roads." Need to preempt by noting terrain-based routing outperforms dome by orders of magnitude, and the Nullarbor flat-terrain counterexample specifically defeats topology-based predictions.

"10,000× larger than standard geodesy" is imprecise. The ratio is ~6,200× vs mm-level GNSS positioning, up to ~250,000× vs sub-mm VLBI. The order-of-magnitude claim is correct but should note the range.
WIN-027 and WIN-057 have null detail_group; WIN-069 has detail_group WIN-027/057/069. Need to update WIN-027 and WIN-057 to also reference the grouping.

12 roads and 9 cross-links cited in detail_claim but our evidence does not challenge for selection bias. With thousands of possible Australian road routes, the cherry-picking concern is real and should be noted.
issues.fix_details: Decider self-apply: added selection bias paragraph to WIN-069 detail_evidence

issues.description: LONGITUDE MISMATCH not mentioned: The Roaring 40s wind belt encircles the entire latitude band continuously. Under dome geometry, southern latitudes compress toward the rim — the circumference at 40°S dome latitude is far shorter than on a globe. A continuous circumpolar wind belt is geometrically impossible on a dome disc where the southern hemisphere rim is stretched. This is the strongest refutation and our review omits it.
issues.fix_details: WIN-024 already contains explicit longitude mismatch argument (SAA at 90°W–40°E vs 360° Roaring 40s). Issue predated existing patch.

issues.fix_details: WIN-031 current text is 2129 chars and covers all major arguments (retrodiction, axis mundi universality, monitor.py absence). The Antarctic ice boundary mention in this issue description is a mislabeling — WIN-031 is North Pole cosmic mountain. Closing as stale.

issues.description: Reconcile data/uncounted-failures.json against the dome's V50.6→V51.1 history. CONTEXT: An audit triggered by integrity report 2026-04-09T08-14 (which flagged WIN-025 as [REMOVED] on the dome site) discovered that the dome_ref field on FAIL-001 through FAIL-005 is completely stale — every W-number we cross-referenced has been REUSED by the dome for an unrelated CONFIRMED prediction (W024: was "Polaris elevation excess", now "Roaring 40s = SAA Southern Boundary CONFIRMED"; W027: was "Schumann G3+ elevation", now "Southern Distance Quadratic Latitude Law CONFIRMED"; W019: was "NMP drift direction", now "Solar Analemma Figure-8 Loop Ratio CONFIRMED"; W020: was "Roaring 40s 500hPa anomaly", now "Lunar Declination 18.6-Year Cycle CONFIRMED"; W021: was "Moon angular diameter variation", now "Gyroscopic Precession Rate CONFIRMED"). The 5 entries are flagged needs_reconciliation:true and excluded from the new headline silent-failures counter until reconciled. TASK: For each of FAIL-001..005, trace the original prediction through V50.6 (raw-text-v50.6-2026-03-12/) and the current site (raw-text/) to determine its current state — is it still on the site under a different W-number? Moved to OPEN-NNN bucket? Marked refined or removed inline? Vanished entirely? FAIL-001 references "OPEN-006 data" suggesting the dome has a separate OPEN-NNN bucket we have not audited — please survey it as part of this work. SCHEMA REDESIGN: The current single dome_ref field cannot capture the full history. Design a new field structure that captures THREE names: (1) current_ref — what the dome calls it now, with "none" as a valid value if vanished; (2) prior_ref — the last known dome reference before reuse; (3) prediction_name — what the prediction was originally registered as (e.g. PROS-003 → WIN-010 promotion path). The dome_status_current field already exists for the silent-failures bucket and can be reused. ROOT CAUSE INVESTIGATION: WIN-058b in our wins.json is suspected to share the same root cause — the dome reused/renumbered around it, and we improvised a sub-id workaround. The integrity report previously flagged a CA_PENDING off-by-one tied to the 058b sub-id. Please investigate whether 058b is another instance of the dome-reuse pattern and whether the new schema would let us remove the workaround. DELIVERABLE: Updated uncounted-failures.json with reconciled entries, new schema applied to all entries, needs_reconciliation flags cleared, and a brief writeup of the WIN-058b investigation. After reconciliation, the headline silent-failures counter on the wins tab will automatically include any reconciled items where current_ref shows the dome no longer counts them.

issues.title: Post-hoc tag: caveat with author self-definition and carve out legitimate novelty
issues.description: Dome author home-page defines prospective as "wrote the prediction down before I pulled the data" — a workflow-local definition, NOT the scientific standard ("observation did not exist at prediction time, or predictor provably had no knowledge of it, with timestamped pre-registration"). We have been holding him to the scientific standard without addressing his stated one. Two required methodology changes: (1) Every WIN rebuttal currently tagged post_hoc: true must explicitly cite the author’s definition and explain why it falls short — not just assert post-hoc. (2) Methodology section needs a caveat paragraph explaining the distinction and defining where we draw the legitimate-novelty line.

position.legitimate_novelty: If the author had a genuinely unique theoretical framework and did NOT happen to know the matching data existed, that is a legitimate independent discovery. Cross-field rediscovery happens constantly in science — researchers in field A don’t know about existing results in field B. Under that scenario, "I wrote it before I pulled the data" IS a valid prospective claim, provided (a) the prediction is non-trivial and specific, and (b) the matching value is not common knowledge or trivially lookup-able.
position.illegitimate_transcription: What is NOT legitimate: predicting a value that is already widely published and trivially lookup-able, then claiming prospective validation because you personally hadn’t run the fetch script yet. Human analogy: "The sky is blue, but I didn’t validate the Pantone; I predict Pantone 2727C; now I validate and yes, it is Pantone 2727C." If the Pantone is in any design textbook, the prediction is transcription, not discovery.
position.the_test: The distinguishing test for each post_hoc WIN: is the specific numerical value in question (a) obscure enough to plausibly be unknown to someone working outside the relevant field, AND (b) specific enough that getting it right is informative? If yes, the claim could be a legitimate independent rediscovery (even if the data existed). If the value is in every physics textbook (Schumann 7.83 Hz, G = 6.67e-11, lunar distance), the prospective claim collapses to transcription.
position.the_smoking_gun: The author’s OWN monitor scripts typically hardcode the expected value in the same file as the live fetch. There is no git-timestamped pre-registration separating prediction from observation. That makes even the author’s weaker self-definition unverifiable — there is no way to prove which came first. Rebuttals should cite this directly when applicable: "You say prospective means before you pulled the data; your code commits show the expected value was added in the same commit as the fetch call, so there is no evidence of prediction preceding observation."

tasks: 1. Write a methodology caveat paragraph for the prose section that currently introduces post_hoc reasoning. Cite the author’s home-page definition verbatim (need URL + quote), explain our stricter standard, define the legitimate-novelty carve-out, define the trivial-transcription test.

2. Audit every WIN currently tagged post_hoc: true. For each, check whether the rebuttal text addresses the author’s definition or just asserts post-hoc. Where it does not, add a sentence citing the author’s definition and explaining why this specific value fails the novelty or pre-registration test.

3. For each post_hoc WIN, classify the value itself: common-knowledge (textbook), field-specific published, obscure. Common-knowledge cases should note that explicitly. Obscure cases may actually strengthen the author’s case — flag for verdict review.

4. Check dome repo git history for genuine pre-registration: are there commits where the expected value was added BEFORE the fetch call was added? If yes, those WINs may deserve different verdict treatment. If no (expected given what we have seen of inject_ai_layer.py etc.), that is a broadly-applicable rebuttal we can cite systematically.
5. Cross-reference with ISS-677 (Prospective Wins Mode 5) — whatever criteria we adopt there must use the same novelty/pre-registration standard this issue establishes.

issues.description: Formally rate kill-shot items using the same verdict taxonomy and analytical fields as WINs. CORE PROBLEM: Our current kill-shot presentation buries the verdict inside prose. A reader has to parse a full paragraph to figure out whether our conclusion on a given kill shot is 'Std Model Explains', 'Self-Contradicted', 'Refuted by Data', etc. The verdict is the tl;dr — it should be visible as a badge, not extracted by close reading. Kill shots currently live in their own tab (since V6.0 restructure) and are presented as curated highlights rather than as individually scored entries. They lack the structured verdict (Refuted by Data / Self-Contradicted / Std Model Explains / Misleading / Not Demonstrated / Unfalsifiable), the code_analysis tags (monitoring / relabels_standard / post_hoc / derives_from_dome), and the other data fields that give WINs a consistent analytical spine. The curmudgeon's per-item review lifecycle also does not apply to them uniformly. TASK: (1) Identify the canonical kill-shot list and current data shape — are they a subset of wins.json, a separate file, or inlined in sections.json? (2) Propose a schema that brings them to parity with WINs: at minimum verdict, finding, detail_evidence, detail_verdict_text, and code_analysis tags. They probably do not need new_in_v51 or detail_group. (3) Apply the schema, assigning a verdict to each kill shot by reading the existing prose and extracting the implicit verdict. Expect a mix — some will be Self-Contradicted or Refuted by Data, but others will land on 'Std Model Explains' (where our point is that the dome's claim adds nothing beyond standard physics) or 'Misleading' (cherry-picked / circular). Do NOT assume all kill shots are Self-Contradicted just because they are the strongest arguments. NEW VERDICT CATEGORY — 'Wait and See' (a.k.a. Legit Pending): introduce this as a valid verdict ONLY in the kill-shot scope for now. It applies when the dome has put forward a proposal that is (a) genuinely unique — not a relabel of standard physics, not already measured — (b) grounded in data the dome has actually produced or can point to, and (c) verifiable in principle, just waiting on time or observation to resolve. This is the honest 'we can't refute this yet, it's a real open prediction' bucket. Important: a Wait-and-See verdict is a compliment — it means the dome has stated something falsifiable and non-trivial. Expect this to be rare; most kill shots will still land in the critical categories. The analyst should be strict about the three criteria — if any one fails, fall back to 'Not Demonstrated' or the appropriate critical verdict. This category will also carry over to ISS-677's Prospective Wins work when that lands, but for now scope it to kill shots only so we don't confuse the headline tallies. Make sure the color/badge for Wait-and-See is visually distinct from the six existing critical verdicts — probably a neutral blue or grey, not the green of 'Std Model Explains'. (4) Update the kill shots tab render to display the verdict badge prominently next to each item, using the same color scheme as the WINs tab. (5) Once tagged, kill shots should enter the curmudgeon review lifecycle so they get adversarial scrutiny on the same cadence. EXPLICITLY NOT IN SCOPE: Kill-shot verdicts should NOT count into the top-line hero tallies or the 4-card scorecard. A kill shot is a curated highlight of an existing argument; double-counting it in the verdict tallies would inflate the numbers. The verdict badge is for reader comprehension, not for aggregation. The existing Kill Shots tab badge is sufficient — no fifth hero card needed. DESIGN QUESTIONS for analyst: Should kill shots remain a separate tab or become a filtered view of WINs with a kill_shot:true flag? If the latter, how do we prevent the filtered view from double-counting in the WINs tab tallies? PRIORITY: Above baseline curmudgeon re-reviews but below ISS-676 (V50.6→V51.1 reconciliation) and ISS-677 (Prospective Wins Mode 5). The reconciliation work may change which kill shots are even valid, so this is better sequenced after ISS-676.

issues.description: VERDICT CATEGORY OVERLOAD: 'Misleading' verdict conflates 4 distinct failure modes: (1) data misrepresentation, (2) duplication/redundancy, (3) non-discrimination (observation explained by multiple models equally), (4) circular derivation. 23 WINs under one label implies systematic dishonesty, but many are simply non-discriminating observations. Consider subcategory 'Non-Discriminating' or move non-discriminating WINs to 'Std Model Explains'. Affects verdict taxonomy coherence and reviewer's interpretation of dome author intent.
issues.rationale_for_deferral: Verdict taxonomy restructuring requires human editorial decision on subcategory definitions
issues.fix_details: Human decision: keep current verdict taxonomy as-is. Splitting Misleading into subcategories risks losing the reader in excessive nuance. The current label is clear enough for a general audience. The curmudgeon observation about non-discriminating WINs is noted but does not warrant restructuring.

issues.description: GitHub PAT stored on disk in workspace FUSE mount at .workspace-sync-token (gitignored, chmod 600). Required so the dome-workspace-sync agent — which runs in its own ephemeral session at a different path — can authenticate pushes without depending on dome-review-clean existing in its session. File is local-only (never committed, verified via git check-ignore and git ls-files), so threat surface is limited to processes running as the workspace user on this machine. Cleanup options to consider when convenient: (a) replace static PAT with a short-lived GitHub App installation token minted on demand, (b) move secret to an OS keychain / credential helper that the agent can call into instead of a flat file, (c) at minimum, rotate the PAT periodically and document the rotation procedure (update .workspace-sync-token AND dome-review-clean/.git/config remote URL, then revoke old PAT). Not urgent — file is not exposed beyond the local VM workspace.
issues.rationale_for_deferral: Secure credential storage requires human decision on approach (GitHub App vs keychain vs rotation policy)
issues.fix_details: Moot: .workspace-sync-token contains a revoked PAT and is no longer used. Workspace-sync now extracts PAT from git remote URL (commit b63de5c). File is harmless dead weight. Long-term credential strategy deferred to if/when needed.

issues.description: SEC-6.4 WIN-024 TAXONOMY MISMATCH: Section 6.4 groups WIN-024 (Roaring 40s) under "Globe Values Relabeled as Dome Predictions" but its code_analysis tag has relabels_standard=false. Curmudgeon notes the inconsistency: a defender could say "even your own tags disagree with your own section grouping." The section labels it "related pattern, false correlation" which is a distinct failure mode from relabeling. Options: (a) move WIN-024 paragraph to a different section (e.g., under non-discriminating correlations, or as a Section 6.6 example); (b) add explicit transition sentence like "A related but distinct pattern is false causal attribution:" to make clear this is a different failure mode.

SEC-6.4 PREEMPTIVE DEFENSE GAP: Curmudgeon Cycle 2 defense_survives=3 for the "relabeling proves too much" objection: "You can't simultaneously demand we explain the same observations as standard physics AND criticize us for explaining the same observations." Also: "30% of WINs don't relabel standard physics — where's your analysis of those?" Analyst should add paragraph to SEC-6.4 directly addressing: (1) relabeling is only a criticism when the new label does no explanatory work — i.e., when the dome framework cannot derive the predicted values from its own parameters and must import standard physics numbers; (2) the 30% non-relabeling WINs are addressed individually in Part 3 where they are found to be refuted by data, self-contradicted, or unfalsifiable. Also incorporate curmudgeon stronger-argument: prominently feature the P-wave shadow zone (WIN-064) as the exemplary case where the dome's mechanism is geometrically incompatible, not just redundant.
issues.notes: defense_survives=3 triggers EXP assignment per decider protocol. Route to analyst as EXP expansion for SEC-6.4 prose addition. Curmudgeon kernel_of_truth: scientific theories do re-explain known phenomena; the criticism is about hollow relabeling that imports others' numbers rather than deriving from dome geometry.

issues.notes:

WIN-025 is the 2024 Eclipse 9-Station test at BOU station (-10 ± 2 nT). Raw-text/02-wins.txt lists wins-page W004 as "Noise Floor Limit (-22.24 nT)" — a different namespace. The prediction values (-10 ± 2 nT) unambiguously identify the eclipse, not the noise-floor. live results.json fetch returned 404 (URL may have changed). issues.description: PRED-NEW-002 (9.2 km/s wave) VERDICT CHALLENGE: Curmudgeon flags our_verdict="unfalsifiable" should be "standard_physics". Value matches known P-wave velocities (8-13 km/s). issues.description: SEC-2.1 ARITHMETIC ERROR: Section says H(15000) ≈ 1,270 km and equatorial f ≈ 59 Hz. Correct values: H(15000) = 8537 × e^(−1.74) = 8537 × 0.17552 = 1,498 km → f = 300000/(4×1498) ≈ 50 Hz. The 15.2% error propagates to the frequency. Additionally, ~22 Hz best estimate is claimed to come from "averaging pole and equatorial heights" — but simple avg of heights gives 14.9 Hz, simple avg of freqs gives 29 Hz. The 22.6 Hz actually comes from the linear radial integral H_eff = H₀·(L/R)·(1−exp(−R/L)) ≈ 3,316 km — valid physics but not described. These errors allow dome defenders to dismiss the entire section. Source: curmudgeon Cycle 2 review of SEC-2.1.

The analyst pipeline does not currently form views on the dome's 10 Prospective Wins (PROS-001..PROS-008 plus WIN-057, WIN-058 which sit in both buckets). Mode 0 (new WIN onboarding) only triggers when the dome's confirmed WIN count exceeds ours, so prospective predictions are invisible to the review pipeline. CONTEXT: The PROS bucket is where the dome stages predictions before promoting them to confirmed wins. Tracking them would let us (a) pre-empt his promotion narrative — when he announces "PROS-001 confirmed, now WIN-070", we already have a critique ready; (b) catch silent suspensions at the prospective level (PROS-003 went SUSPENDED in the 2026-03-15 batch alongside WIN-010/WIN-025 and we missed it until today); (c) score his prospective hit rate over time as a separate accuracy figure that is actually predictive rather than retrodictive. PROPOSAL: Add a new analyst Mode 5 (prospective tracking) and a new data file data/prospective.json with one entry per PROS item. Each entry would capture: prediction text, stated test conditions, our pre-emptive critique, current dome status (active / promoted / suspended / removed), and link to the WIN entry if/when promoted. The poller already fetches the prospective section, so the data is available; just needs a Mode 5 to act on it. The new headline structure on the wins tab could grow a fifth metric for prospective hit rate once Mode 5 has produced enough data. DESIGN DECISIONS for analyst: should prospective items live in a separate file or as a section of wins.json? Should there be a new tab on the review site, or just additional rows in the WINs tab? Should the silent-failures counter pull in suspended-prospective items (currently FAIL-008 is the only one, manually backfilled today)?

issues.description: SEC-2.1 STRUCTURAL WEAKNESS: Section front-loads weakest argument (~22 Hz avg) and buries strongest argument (supremum impossibility: H(r) ≤ 8537 km, so 9572 km and 9086 km are mathematically impossible). Curmudgeon recommends: lead with supremum impossibility, then formula-switching critique, then use H_eff integrals as supplementary detail. Also: the monotonicity argument (f_QW(r) is monotonically increasing from 8.77 Hz to 89.5 Hz, equals 7.83 Hz at NO real radius) is stronger than any averaging. Q-factor argument still absent: Schumann Q ≈ 4-6 requires lossy ionospheric boundary, copper dome gives Q >> 100. Source: curmudgeon Cycle 2 review of SEC-2.1.

issues.title: SEC-6.8 mathematical error: two-pole formula does NOT collapse to single exponential when r_N ≠ r_S
issues.description: Section 6.8 claims: if both terms use the same decay constant and r_N and r_S are not independent (r_S = 2r_eq - r_N), the formula is a single exponential. This is false when r_N ≠ r_S — e^(-r_N/λ) + e^(-r_S/λ) with r_S=R-r_N is NOT e^(-r/λ) × constant. The valid critique is 3 free parameters (A, C, λ) are all fitted to data with no derivation from dome geometry. Curmudgeon review SEC-6.8.c2.json and SEC-3.5.8.json both flag this.

The "wrong sign" claim requires comparing to what the dome actually predicted, not to zero. The paragraph needs to state the dome-predicted direction and show the observation contradicts it.

issues.title: SEC-1.8 missing chromaticity bridge for Gaia argument
issues.description: SEC-1.8 Gaia paragraph argues n(r)>>1 invalidates Gaia astrometry but does not mention that refraction is chromatic. A smart dome defender could argue: "if n(r) bends ALL light uniformly, Gaia sees self-consistent positions even through the medium." The preemption needed: refraction is inherently chromatic (Kramers-Kronig relation: any n>1 medium must be dispersive). Gaia measures positions at multiple wavelengths simultaneously; a chromatic medium would produce wavelength-dependent position offsets detectable in the data. The dome's n(r) specification provides no dispersion function, making its medium physically impossible AND Gaia's achromatic catalog a direct falsification.

SEC-1.1 TLDR ends with "the review structure, not the geometry, is what makes it interesting to evaluate." issues.title: HOL-STRESSTEST: No explicit success category defined — what would a genuine dome WIN look like?

If the dome model produced a genuinely discriminating prediction, would we recognize it? The Evaluation Guide defines the standard, but no WIN has ever been shown to meet it, and we never explicitly tell the reader what evidence would change our verdict. This makes the review vulnerable to "moving the goalposts" accusations.

issues.title: HOL-STRESSTEST: Std Model Explains (24%) conflates two distinct situations — globe-compatible vs globe-predicted
issues.description: HOL-STRESSTEST hole 1: The "Std Model Explains" verdict conflates: (a) dome prediction matches observation AND matches standard physics prediction (dome and globe predict same — no discrimination possible), and (b) dome prediction matches observation but standard physics ALSO explains it via a different mechanism (dome is compatible but not uniquely supported). These are different epistemic situations and should be clearly distinguished to avoid a defender claiming our taxonomy is unfair.

KILLSHOT-GAIA cycle 1: Missing explicit error analysis. Section asserts dome fails Gaia parallax but does not compute expected dome parallax from its geometry and compare to Gaia catalog values. Also missing: why Gaia internal consistency is incompatible with flat-disc geometry (solar system parallax would be entirely wrong).

issues.description: SEC-5 Test 6 (NMP Drift Rate) verdict paragraph states "(b) exceeds the dome's own 30% falsification threshold" — but the dome's actual threshold is ">50% for 3 consecutive years" (confirmed raw-text/08-live-power.txt line 65). The TLDR and body paragraph correctly say 50%. 39.9% does NOT exceed 50%, so the verdict is factually wrong AND self-contradictory (TLDR/body=50%, verdict=30%). This was missed during EXP-082 integration. Source: SEC-5-post-EXP082.c3.json (reviewed 2026-04-13).

SEC-5 Test 6 body paragraph has duplicate opening sentence: "The dome's own tracking shows failure. The dome's own tracking shows substantial error." — build/merge artifact from EXP-082 integration. The first sentence ("shows failure") contradicts the body text (dome says PASS at 39.9%). Delete the first sentence, keep only "The dome's own tracking shows substantial error." Source: SEC-5-post-EXP082.c3.json (reviewed 2026-04-13).

SEC-5 opening paragraph says "one (NMP drift rate) self-reports as failing by its own 39.9% error metric" — but the dome says PASS (39.9% is within its 50% tolerance). The summary table correctly shows "PASS (39.9%)". The opening prose contradicts the table. Fix to: "one (NMP drift rate) self-reports as passing at 39.9% error — though only by widening the tolerance to 50% after predictions missed." Source: SEC-5-post-EXP082.c3.json (reviewed 2026-04-13).

WIN-065 detail_evidence uses wrong n(r) formula "n(r) = 1 + 0.20·(r/20,015)²" (quadratic) when computing refraction at Oslo. The dome's actual formula is n(r) = 1 + 0.20 × (8537/H(r) − 1) (exponential, based on H(r) scale height). The quadratic gives n=1.20 at ice wall; the correct formula gives n=2.84. The refraction estimate (~0.04° at Oslo) remains approximately correct with the right formula (n(2753)≈1.075, δθ≈0.043° at elevation 59.9°), but the formula citation is scientifically wrong and exploitable. Section 1.8 correctly uses the dome formula. Source: SEC-1.8-post-EXP129.c2.json (reviewed 2026-04-13).

SEC-1.8 states "The dome's r(city) formula maps Polaris elevation to radial distance exactly as an azimuthal equidistant projection maps globe latitude to radial distance on a flat map." The H(r)≈AE correspondence is approximate (ratio 0.96–1.94, good in 30°–60° band, diverges at extremes), not exact. "Exactly as" overstates the correspondence and creates a vulnerability for dome defenders. Fix: change to "analogously to how" or "in the same way that." Source: SEC-1.8-post-EXP129.c2.json (reviewed 2026-04-13).

WIN-062 verdict is Misleading (relabeling globe circumference as disc diameter) — not a circularity argument. PRED-TOROID-003: Reasoning does not explicitly state that even if 0.336 Hz falls near observed splitting ranges (0.1-0.3 Hz), the derivation is still invalid because the dome Schumann fundamental is ~22 Hz (not 7.83 Hz). Perturbation theory built on the wrong base mode produces splitting of the wrong fundamental. Closes a potential dome defender escape hatch.

PRED-CURR-008: detail_reasoning establishes the partially-correct subset first (mid-latitude westerlies = standard physics), then states the contradicted part (single global rotation contradicted by trade winds). Leading with the falsification angle (multi-cell structure directly contradicts single-rotation claim) makes the standard_physics verdict clearer.

PRED-batch-EXP-085: Optics risk of 11 recycled verdicts in a single batch. A reader seeing 11 predictions labeled recycled without context may suspect dismissiveness. The per-prediction detail_reasoning panels exist and render, but the predictions section prose should explicitly acknowledge the thoroughness of per-prediction analysis. Add a brief prose note in the recycled/post-hoc section explaining that each entry was individually evaluated.

issues.description: SEC-2b.7 W001 sign-flip paragraph: verbatim quote "Instrument noise floor prevents measurement. Prediction remains structurally valid. Hardware limitation, not model failure." cites dome as source but no URL/commit/date footnote. Raw-text archive has shorter formulations that do not match verbatim. If dome updates wins.html, a defender could claim misquotation. Either add a capture-date footnote or replace with paraphrase from raw-text.

SEC-6.1 WIN-013 argument sharpener: Section says "If the dome formula reproduces WGS84, one of two things is true: either the dome independently derived the same result from different physics (no derivation shown), or the dome formula was fitted to WGS84 values." A defender can counter: "gravity measurements are geometry-independent." Recommend acknowledging this and responding: if the dome formula reproduces WGS84 regardless of underlying geometry, then the formula carries no discriminating power between globe and dome models.

SEC-2b.7 W001 paragraph cites sigma_distance 7.3 relative to ≈0.8 nT prediction uncertainty, but the source of the 0.8 nT figure is not given. The dome's results.json sigma_distance is calculated from (obs - pred) / sigma_pred, where sigma_pred is the dome's own prediction precision. Our text should cite the exact dome-file field where this denominator comes from to prevent the dome saying our sigma calculation is wrong.
issues.assignment_note: Curmudgeon review (c2) found 4 holes: sigma_distance/SNR conflation, missing noise floor value, unpreempted dome defense, imprecise overrides language. Requires structural reframe: stop claiming 7-sigma detection of wrong answer; instead argue predictions are unfalsifiable-by-construction because designed to sit below noise floor. See reviews/part2b-W001-signflip.c2.json for full recommendation.
issues.fix_note: Staleness: 0.8 nT figure source now provided via precision derivation in EXP-137 rewrite text

issues.title: SEC-3.5.1: B(r) formula uses single-variable r instead of r_N/r_S; κ coupling constant attributed to wrong storms
issues.description: Two major errors in Section 3.5.1: (1) B(r) formula written as 62,376×e^(−r/8619) + 64,852×e^(−r/8619) using single variable r for both terms, then incorrectly claims this simplifies to 127,228. The correct formula uses r_N and r_S (distance from North/South pole respectively) — these are not the same and do not sum to 127,228. (2) κ coupling constant 10.9 nT attributed to "2003 Halloween storm" but dome WIN-012 explicitly states this comes from BOU 2017 solar eclipse (10.9 nT) and 1997 Mohe eclipse (6.5 μGal) — different events, different physics. Both are factual errors requiring prose rewrite.

(b) Fix κ attribution from Halloween storm to BOU 2017 + Mohe 1997 eclipses per WIN-012. Cross-check with sections 3.5.8 which already uses correct dual-variable formula.

issues.title: KILLSHOT-GAIA: star table labeled "Gaia observations" but Sirius/Vega/Betelgeuse/Deneb values are Hipparcos
issues.description: The stellar parallax kill-shot table is labeled "Gaia observations" but at least 4 of 5 parallax values appear to be from Hipparcos (van Leeuwen 2007), not Gaia DR3. Sirius (379.21 ± 1.58 mas), Vega (130.23 ± 0.36 mas), Betelgeuse (4.51 ± 0.80 mas), and Deneb (1.01 ± 0.22 mas) are all in the bright-star regime where Gaia standard astrometric pipeline cannot measure reliably due to CCD saturation. These values match the Hipparcos new reduction catalog. Only Proxima Centauri (768.07 ± 0.05 mas) is clearly from Gaia DR3. Fix: either (a) change table header to "Observed parallax (Gaia DR3 / Hipparcos)" and note source per star, or (b) replace bright-star entries with fainter Gaia-only stars (Barnard's Star 546.98 mas, 61 Cygni A 287.18 mas, Ross 154 336.72 mas). Option (b) is stronger — eliminates the attack surface and gives even more extreme sigma values. Content is in sections.json part5.
issues.fix_details: Table header already reads Gaia DR3 / Hipparcos in current sections.json. Issue is stale — already fixed by queue item 98 (KILLSHOT-GAIA-table-citation-fix).

issues.title: KILLSHOT-GAIA: "~25° at mid-elevation" refraction escape hatch needs geometry specification
issues.description: The refraction escape hatch paragraph claims angular displacements "~25° at mid-elevation" for n=1.44. This is an order-of-magnitude estimate that depends on viewing geometry. At 40° from zenith with n=1.44, displacement is ~28°; total internal reflection occurs beyond ~44°. Recommends: "For n(r)=1.44, a ray at 40° from zenith would be refracted by ~28°, and total internal reflection occurs beyond 44° from zenith." This makes the point more devastating (TIR at moderate elevation angles). KILLSHOT-GAIA-v2.c2.json hole 4.

Source: SEC-5-post-ISS860-861-862.c4

Part 5 NMP body and TLDR reference '<20 km/yr by 2027' target but this cannot be found in dome's published material. Dome's own formula gives 21.06 km/yr for 2027 — above 20. The formula doesn't cross <20 until mid-2028. We may be critiquing a prediction the dome never made. Fix: replace with dome's actual formula prediction (~21 km/yr for 2027). Source: SEC-5-post-ISS860-861-862.c4

Part 5 closing summary says 'One test is failing by nearly 40%' but opening paragraph (correctly) says dome 'self-reports as passing at 39.9% error.' Fix closing: 'One test shows 39.9% error — counted as PASS only via a widened tolerance and a three-year consecutive requirement that allows single-year escapes.' Source: SEC-5-post-ISS860-861-862.c4

Part 5 closing summary characterizes Polaris as 'within measurement noise' but omits that dome's own site marks broader Polaris-latitude prediction as FALSIFIED (FAIL-001). Self-falsification is stronger than noise-floor argument. Add: 'within measurement noise (Polaris: claimed excess smaller than the star's own polar offset, and the dome's broader Polaris-latitude prediction is self-marked FALSIFIED)'. Source: SEC-5-post-ISS860-861-862.c4

Poller: dome site has not recorded any result (no WIN count change, no accuracy shift). Decider should investigate whether the G3+ storm occurred and whether SR data shows the predicted suppression, then update our_verdict in predictions.json.

issues.fix_details: EXP-157 applied: PRED-SR-SUPPRESS detail_reasoning updated with inductive reasoning defense, specific Rosales-Linares DOI citation, and Domain 9 discrepancy note. tldr updated.

Text says this comes from GR predictions but does not cite the primary source (e.g., GPS ICD or Ashby 2002 Liv. Rev. Rel.). A hostile reader can claim this is a round number or approximation and demand the calculation. Source: KILLSHOT-GPS.json cycle 1.

issues.description: PRED-SR-SUPPRESS and PRED-093 assessment updates fail to note that the dome's own monitoring Dashboard (Domain 9: Schumann Amplitude Suppression) reports "Unscored — no G3+ storm observed" during the March 20-22 period, despite NOAA SWPC confirming G3 levels (Kp reached 7). Either a monitor bug or selective non-scoring. Both are significant: a monitor that misses G3 storms undermines the automation narrative; selective non-scoring raises scoring integrity questions. This observation should be added to both prediction assessments.
issues.fix_details: Domain 9 discrepancy already resolved in PRED-SR-SUPPRESS and PRED-093 detail_reasoning (updated 2026-04-14). Per proposal-ISS-933-resolution.json.

issues.title: SEC-1.8: Missing defense preemption — n(r) empirically fitted to reproduce correct Polaris elevations
issues.description: Curmudgeon c2 review found that Section 1.8 does not address the defense that n(r) was empirically fitted to reproduce correct Polaris elevations AND that this is the right methodology for dome physics. Need to add to the "other side" section. The response: even if n(r) is fitted to Polaris, the SAME function must also correct for solar distance — and it produces 52% solar diameter variation instead of 0.01%. Fitting to one observable while failing another is model failure, not success.
issues.fix_details: EXP-149 integrated into SEC-1.8: n(r) empirical fitting defense paragraph added after wavelength-selectivity defense

issues.description: SEC-5 (Part 5 Kill Shots) closing summary bullets omit the Polaris FAIL-001 self-falsification. issues.description: Part 1 evidence-roadmap div: Schumann frequency stated as "22–35 Hz" (the dome model range) but Section 2.1 heading reads "~22 Hz". A reader clicking through from the roadmap sees a different number. Either use ~22 Hz (matching the section heading, linear average) or update Section 2.1 heading to show the full range 22–35 Hz.

issues.description: SEC-5 Test 1 body text says "The V12 Finsler formula produced a -78% error" but V12 did not use Finsler geometry — Finsler was introduced in V13. Should read "V12 coordinate system" or "V12 exponential H(r) coordinate system".

issues.description: KILLSHOT-SH (Southern Hemisphere kill-shot): The SYD-EZE V12 error claim of '-78%' is stated but the derivation is not shown inline. A dome defender can challenge the number and we have no inline calculation to point to. The derivation should be shown or cited to our own data files. Source: KILLSHOT-SH.json cycle 1.
issues.fix_details: EXP-131 integrated: SYD-EZE V12 error derivation added inline. Error corrected from -78% to -73% (dome own coordinates). Dome admission cited. Three free parameters renamed to structural elements. Computed distance corrected from ~2,600 km to 3,194 km.

issues.description: SEC-1.3 TLDR/body disconnect: TLDR makes strong dependency argument (depends, not competes) but body has zero evidence for it. Section title says How It Differs from Globe Model but body only describes globe — no comparison to dome architecture. Needs: (1) 1-2 sentences in body grounding TLDR dependency claim with OPEN-001/Polaris-as-latitude evidence; (2) brief dome vs globe comparison. Minor: Keplerian mechanics understates GPS correction (should include relativistic). Minor: ionosphere range 80-400 km misses D layer at 60 km.

WIN-065 detail_evidence has orphaned sentence fragment: "The dome's PRED-NEW-001, registered 2026-03-12," followed immediately by a new sentence. The fragment has no predicate — it is a remnant from when a duplicate paragraph was removed (c2 cycle). Delete the fragment.

WIN-065 detail_evidence: (1) ~0.04° refraction figure has likely unit error — standard formula R=(n-1)cot(h) gives 0.0436 RADIANS = 2.50 DEGREES at h=59.9°, not 0.04°. Factor-of-57 error. (2) Sentence attributes formula as "The dome's own refractive index formula" but this formula does not appear in dome raw text — dome never explicitly states n(r) = 1 + 0.20×(8537/H(r)−1). Fix: remove specific refraction number, replace with Section 1.8 cascade argument (safer, model-independent). Change attribution to "The refractive index implied by the dome's H(r) function".

EXP-054-batch.c2: V-template mismatch for Unfalsifiable/theological WINs (WIN-023, WIN-032, WIN-034). The batch audit appended sentences using the V-template which opens with 'The dome's original test was...' but unfalsifiable WINs have no testable prediction to reference. The template sentence is logically incoherent for these WIN types. The appended sentences for unfalsifiable WINs need a different template. Source: EXP-054-batch.c2.json.
issues.resolution: Fixed prior to this run. EXP-132 confirmed all three WINs (023, 032, 034) already use appropriate U-category text in detail_verdict_text. No patch needed.

issues.description: EVAL-GUIDE (Evaluation Guide section): Principle 3 claims 'Mainstream physics is not assumed correct by default' but the evaluation methodology uses mainstream-derived measurements as the reference standard for all comparisons. The guide cannot simultaneously claim neutrality on mainstream physics and then compare dome predictions against NOAA magnetometry, Gaia astrometry, and GPS timing. The principle should be reworded to: 'We do not assume the dome's predictions are wrong simply because they differ from mainstream cosmology' — a more limited and accurate claim. Source: EVAL-GUIDE.json cycle 1.
issues.resolution: Applied PATCH-1 and PATCH-1b from EXP-133. Principle 3 heading reworded from "Mainstream physics is not assumed correct by default" to "Claims are evaluated against measurement, not against theoretical authority." Added explicit acknowledgment of measurement infrastructure asymmetry. TLDR updated to match.

issues.title: Falsifiability section: eclipse discrimination window is only 1 nT — worth acknowledging
issues.description: The eclipse pre-commitment paragraph (generate-html.js eg-falsifiability) claims the dome predicts -17 to -21 nT while Chapman predicts 5-20 nT. The discriminating window is only 20-21 nT (1 nT) — any measurement ≤20 nT is a win for both frameworks. The text does not acknowledge this narrow window, which makes the upgrade condition seem more decisive than it is. SEC-overview-scorecard.c3.json hole 2.
issues.staleness_note: generate-html.js infrastructure — cannot patch directly; needs EXP or human edit
issues.resolution: Applied PATCH-2 from EXP-133. Eclipse pre-commitment paragraph now acknowledges the ~1 nT discrimination window and frames narrowness as evidence of derivative methodology.

issues.description: SEC-5 Test 6 TLDR historical deceleration rate uses peak ~55 km/yr estimate, giving ~0.77 km/yr/yr and a ~23× ratio. Livermore et al. (2020) documents peak drift speed of ~60 km/yr in 2003-2005: (60-38)/22 = 1.0 km/yr/yr, giving ratio of ~18×. The TLDR's ~55 km/yr estimate produces a more dramatic ratio than the peer-reviewed figure warrants. Fix: use Livermore et al. (2020) figure or cite both as "roughly 18–23×". Does not affect verdict — 40% error is damning regardless of this ratio.
issues.resolution: Applied direct patch to sections.json part5: updated peak NMP drift rate from ~55 km/yr to ~60 km/yr (Livermore et al. 2020, 2003-2005 peak) with citation added inline.

issues.description: FAIL-004 (SEC-6.x): The equivalence claim '≥+1.5σ threshold that would correspond to a ≥3% anomaly' conflates AAO index sigma (standardized anomaly) with the Roaring Forties wind percentage. The AAO index σ unit is not directly proportional to a percentage wind anomaly. The cited ≥3% threshold for the dome's prediction applies to wind speed/pressure, not to AAO index units. Either show the conversion explicitly or remove the threshold equivalence. Source: part6-FAIL-004.c2.json.
issues.fix_details: EXP-134 paragraph replacement applied to part6 FAIL-004: removed unjustified AAO σ↔500hPa% equivalence, added NOAA CPC citation, noted pressure-level mismatch.

issues.description: FAIL-004 (SEC-6.x): No citation for the +0.78σ AAO value stated as the outcome during the prediction window. The text mentions 'NCEP AAO data' but the specific +0.78σ value lacks a DOI, dataset URL, or data archive reference. Should cite the NOAA Climate Prediction Center AAO data archive with the specific file/date range used. Source: part6-FAIL-004.c2.json.
issues.fix_details: EXP-134 paragraph replacement applied to part6 FAIL-004: removed unjustified AAO σ↔500hPa% equivalence, added NOAA CPC citation, noted pressure-level mismatch.

issues.description: SEC-6.3 (old SEC-3.5.3 — dome parallel-plate Schumann critique): Section asserts 'the dome's parallel-plate cavity geometry actually predicts Schumann frequencies at X Hz' but does not show the derivation. A dome defender or reader cannot verify the predicted frequency without seeing the calculation using dome parameters (firmament height 8,537 km). Should add a footnote showing f = c/(2H) for a parallel-plate cavity using H from the dome's own parameters. Source: SEC-3.5.3.json cycle 1.

SEC-8.11: "The dome's homepage summarizes the prediction as −17 to −21 nT" — this range does not appear in raw-text/01-home.txt, 02-wins.txt, or 03-predictions.txt. May be from an older site version or may be incorrect. Either verify against live dome homepage and add citation, or replace with: "The dome's predictions span −5 to −26 nT across two baselines and 9 stations" (dropping the unverifiable homepage attribution). Source: SEC-8.11.c1.json.

SEC-8.11: Sign convention inconsistency — dome predictions use negative values (−5 to −26 nT representing dips below baseline) while Chapman range is stated as positive (5–25 nT). Both represent the same physical quantity but mixed signs could confuse readers. Add "absolute magnitude" or "|ΔZ|" parenthetical, or use consistent notation: "The conservative baseline (5–13 nT in absolute terms) falls within the Chapman range." AFFECTS SUMMARY TABLE. Source: SEC-8.11.c1.json.

SEC-8.11: "The Chapman Sq-current mechanism predicts 5–25 nT under identical conditions" — "identical conditions" oversells specificity. The range is an empirical finding across many eclipses at various stations and latitudes, not a specific prediction for this event. Replace "under identical conditions" with "across comparable eclipse conditions" or "in the published eclipse literature." AFFECTS SUMMARY TABLE. Source: SEC-8.11.c1.json.

SEC-2.4: "orbiting the pole at radius ≈ 7,400 km" has no source in this section. Section 7.10 clarifies it as the Tropic of Cancer in dome coordinates, but SEC-2.4 standalone readers have no context. Add parenthetical: "(the Tropic of Cancer in dome coordinates — the most favorable case for the dome, as the equinox orbit at ~11,000 km would produce even greater variation)". Source: SEC-2.4.c2.json.

SEC-2.4: Cross-reference to Section 7.10 uses href="#part7" with onclick showTab("falsify"), which navigates to Part 7 top rather than Section 7.10 specifically. Change href to "#p7-710-9" (actual anchor for Section 7.10) so clicking navigates directly. Source: SEC-2.4.c2.json.

WIN-058b: The r=0.96 vs r=0.992 "inconsistency" argument has an unaddressed defense. Dome could argue r=0.96 measures κ slope-fit correlation while r=0.992 measures raw temporal cross-correlation of two different statistics from the same data. Either (a) investigate whether dome defines these differently and rebut explicitly, or (b) soften to: "the dome cites two different r-values (0.96 and 0.992) from the same data without specifying what each measures — neither is computed by any repository script the dome publishes." Source: WIN-058b-post-EXP102.c2.json.

WIN-058b: SNR framing has rhetorical vulnerability. Text says "Even at the absurdly conservative lower bound of SNR = 5" but earlier states SG signals have SNR ≥ 50. A defender can question why we use SNR=5 as our anchor if we claim SNR=50 is realistic. Fix: lead with physically realistic SNR = 10–50 range and its r > 0.99 results, then add SNR = 5 as a brief robustness footnote rather than the centerpiece. Source: WIN-058b-post-EXP102.c2.json.

WIN-058b: detail_evidence opens with the numbering collision bookkeeping note instead of the scientific argument. Readers expanding the detail see admin content before science. Move numbering collision to a brief parenthetical at the end. Lead with "The κ constant is not derived from dome geometry" — the strongest scientific argument. Source: WIN-058b-post-EXP102.c2.json.

issues.title: W019 (NMP poleward drift): our_verdict should be "falsified" not "standard_physics"
issues.description: W019 (NMP drift poleward dominates) currently has our_verdict="standard_physics". Curmudgeon challenges: the dome predicted poleward drift DOMINATES; observations show lateral drift is 5.5x larger. The dome author himself marked it REFINED (conceding failure). We are more charitable to the dome than the dome author. Curmudgeon recommends: restore verdict to "falsified". Source: curmudgeon review of PRED-batch-2026-04-13.
issues.notes: Verdict change requires human approval. Curmudgeon notes the dome author himself conceded failure (REFINED status).

issues.title: W020 (Roaring 40s ≥3%): our_verdict should be "falsified" not "unfalsifiable"
issues.description: W020 (Roaring 40s 500 hPa anomaly ≥3%) currently has our_verdict="unfalsifiable". Curmudgeon challenges: the prediction specified a concrete ≥3% threshold. The author conceded the observed effect is smaller than 3%. A prediction with a quantitative criterion that fails that criterion is FALSIFIED, not unfalsifiable. The current detail_reasoning says "no clear test criterion" but ≥3% IS a clear criterion — internal inconsistency in our own text. Source: curmudgeon review of PRED-batch-2026-04-13.
issues.notes: Verdict change requires human approval. Detail_reasoning claims "no clear test criterion" but ≥3% IS the criterion — needs correction either way.

issues.title: PRED-NEW-002 (aetheric gravity wave 9.2 km/s): should be "standard_physics" not "unfalsifiable"
issues.description: PRED-NEW-002 (Aetheric gravity wave 9.2 km/s) currently has our_verdict="unfalsifiable". Curmudgeon challenges: 9.2 km/s falls within the P-wave velocity range (8–13 km/s per PREM model). This IS testable and matches standard seismology. Calling it unfalsifiable gives the dome an unearned grievance — it implies we cannot evaluate the claim, when in fact it matches known physics. Source: curmudgeon review of PRED-batch-2026-04-13.
issues.notes: Verdict change requires human approval. If 9.2 km/s = P-wave speed, correct verdict is standard_physics (dome unwittingly predicts observed seismology).

issues.description: PRED-TOROID-003 our_verdict should change from unfalsifiable to standard_physics. Analyst EXP-136 confirmed: prediction IS testable (curmudgeon correct) but dome derivation has two fatal internal contradictions: (1) stated eccentricity e=0.66 gives ratio 0.142, not the claimed 0.0215 (6.6× error); (2) derivation uses globe 7.83 Hz not dome own ~22 Hz. data_predates_registration already changed to true. Verdict change requires human approval.

issues.title: PRED-TOROID-003 (Schumann Δf ≈ 0.336 Hz) is unclassified but appears genuinely testable — should get our_verdict
issues.description: From ISS-703-sha256-forensics.c2.json: PRED-TOROID-003 (Schumann mode splitting Δf ≈ 0.336 Hz) is currently unclassified (our_verdict=null). The curmudgeon notes this is genuinely novel — the toroidal cavity geometry implies different mode structure from a spherical cavity, and the predicted frequency split is quantitatively specific and testable. It should be reclassified as testable_prediction or similar, not left null alongside retrospective citations.
issues.pending_note: EXP-136 complete. detail_reasoning and tldr applied. our_verdict change ISS-954 awaits human approval.

issues.title: WIN-058 verdict: 'Misleading' may not fit — not cherry-picked/duplicated/circular
issues.description: HOLISTIC-HOL-TAXONOMY C2: WIN-058 (Unified angular coordinate θ = −lonE) verdict is Misleading but the finding is not cherry-picking/duplicating/circularity — it's that the dome fixed a broken proxy and claims it as a discovery. A more accurate verdict might be "Not Demonstrated" (no claim beyond coordinate definition) or "Std Model Explains" (it just IS geographic longitude). Needs human adjudication.

Part 1 roadmap Tier 3 framing apologizes for our own evidence: 'that a determined defender could dispute as measured from the wrong frame.' This hands ammunition to dome defenders. Restructure to lead with Tier 1 strength rather than Tier 3 vulnerability. Needs prose rewrite. Source: part1-evidence-roadmap.c1

SEC-2b.1 stratification paragraph uses hardcoded count words ("Seven", "Fifteen", "Twelve", "Three", "Two") for sub-breakdowns of the CA_NONE unmonitored WINs by verdict category. These should be template variables or at minimum noted as point-in-time counts. As WINs are added or verdicts change, these hardcoded words become stale and self-contradict our criticism of the dome for manually-entered statistics.
issues.fix_details: EXP-158 fallback applied: added caveat text to SEC-2b.1 stratification paragraph indicating counts are from wins.json at build time. Full template-variable wiring requires generate-html.js changes (out of scope for patch file; tracked as follow-up).

issues.description: WIN-035 detail_verdict_text (EXP-062 batch): claims PROS-005 was registered on 1 January 2026. Dome source raw-text/02-wins.txt states 2026-03-06. Critical self-introduced error that a defender would use to discredit the OTS critique.

WIN-037 detail_verdict_text (EXP-062 batch): claims PROS-007 registered 1 January 2026 and includes Credit-where-due concession. Dome source states 2026-03-06 (final week of window). At registration ~99% of decay had occurred. Concession reverses under correct date.

WIN-039 detail_verdict_text: editorial speculation that dome adopted value without understanding oscillating signal nature. Unsupported claim opens attack surface. Replaced with factual description of unreconciled discrepancy.
WIN-036 detail_verdict_text: overstates WIN-007 relationship as logical entailment. WIN-007 predicts pole acceleration rate, not position. Changed to trivial consequence framing.
SEC-3 roadmap (EXP-116): outside in framing is structurally inaccurate — Part 5 tests the dome own binary criteria (internal test). Replaced with escalation description.

SEC-3 roadmap (EXP-116): Each level is independent claim is overreached — Part 3 and Part 4 are not independent (Part 4 derives from Part 3 verdict counts). Replaced with accurate dependence structure.

SEC-4 roadmap (EXP-116): Part 3 showed individual claims fail under scrutiny collapses heterogeneous verdicts (24% Std-Model-Explains are not failures). Also missing independent force framing for the shared-lambda_g finding. Both fixed.

SEC-5 Kill-Shot Binary Tests: Strength rating key states "Current ratings: 3 decisive, 2 major, 2 supportive" but individual test ratings are not visible in the text — the reader cannot verify which tests received which rating, making the count an unsupported assertion. Either add rating badges to individual test headers or remove the assertion.

issues.description: SEC-5 Test 5 (Eclipse 2026) is a single-paragraph stub that defers entirely to Section 4.2. issues.fix_details: EXP-159 patches applied: PATCH-1 through PATCH-9 (strength rating badges + Test 5 Eclipse expansion)

issues.description: SEC-8.11 eclipse prediction analysis cites dome homepage as source for -17 to -21 nT prediction range, but this claim does not appear in any raw-text archive file (checked 01-home.txt through 08-live-power).
